name: Zesty Green Split Peas
recipeInstructions:
If black mustard seeds are not available, use 1 tablespoon dry mustard.
Mix the ground mustard powder with water and allow it to sit for 30 minutes.
Bring split peas and water to a boil. Lower heat to medium. Add bay leaves and turmeric (I forgot the turmeric--it was good anyway). Simmer, covered, until peas are done, 40 to 45 minutes. (When pressed between the thumb and index finger, they break easily.)
While simmering, uncover occasionally and stir, adding a tablespoon or so of hot water if the mixture sticks to the bottom of the pan.
When cooked, remove and discard bay leaves. Add salt and keep warm.
Heat oil in a small, 6-inch skillet over medium-low heat. Saute ginger root and jalapenos until ginger is lightly browned, about 1 to 2 minutes. Add mustard paste and cook for another minute, stirring constantly. Add coconut and stir several times. Remove from heat.
Pour over the pea mixture and stir. (I had to drain my peas, because I still had lots of water in them.) Let stand covered for 15 minutes.
Add a dab of soy margarine if desired and garnish with cilantro. (I didn't use the cilantro)
I think this would make a good filling for pita pockets. I didn't think the peas were "zesty" enough, so I sprinkled hot pepper vinegar on mine.
Per serving: 113 calories; 5g protein; 5g fat (with 2 tablespoons of oil); 13g carbohydrates; 0 cholesterol; 274mg sodium; 3g fiber.
